evals.prompt: I have a .NET MAUI app using ZXing.Net.Maui with Multiple = true to scan all visible barcodes on screen. I want to replace it with MatrixScan Batch. Every visible barcode (EAN-13, Code 128, QR) should keep being tracked, deduplicated by value, and accumulated into a list. Here is my page: ZxingMauiBatchPage.xaml + ZxingMauiBatchPage.xaml.cs.
evals.expected_output: Removes the ZXing.Net.Maui XAML control, namespace, options, and BarcodesDetected event handler. Replaces with <scandit:DataCaptureView> + BarcodeBatch mode + HandlerChanged BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay creation. Maps the ZXing formats (Ean13, Code128, QrCode) to Scandit symbologies (Ean13Upca, Code128, Qr). Preserves the ScannedBarcode record and deduplication logic, ideally translating manual Value-based dedupe to tracking-ID-based dedupe via session.AddedTrackedBarcodes + a HashSet<int> of Identifiers. OnSessionUpdated dispatches UI updates via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread inside a try/finally with frameData.Dispose().

assertions.text: ZXing.Net.Maui using directives are NOT present in the new code
<zxing:CameraBarcodeReaderView is NOT present in the updated XAML (may appear in a before/after summary table)
The xmlns:zxing namespace is NOT present in the updated XAML
BarcodeReaderOptions is NOT present in the new code
BarcodesDetected event handler signature is NOT present in the new code
Scandit.DataCapture using directives ARE present
<scandit:DataCaptureView> is present in the updated XAML
The xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditCaptureCoreMaui" namespace is added
<scandit:DataCaptureView> sets DataCaptureContext="{Binding DataCaptureContext}" (or equivalent binding to a DataCaptureContext)
The view model implements IBarcodeBatchListener (or subscribes to barcodeBatch.SessionUpdated +=)
OnSessionUpdated iterates session.AddedTrackedBarcodes (the new-this-frame collection)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
Symbology.Qr is enabled (not Symbology.QrCode, which does not exist)
Symbology.QrCode does NOT appear as a Symbology enum value (BarcodeFormat.QrCode in a summary table is acceptable)
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.Create(barcodeBatch ...) is called inside dataCaptureView.HandlerChanged (single-argument or barcodeBatch+style overload, not the native-view overload)
dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(overlay) is called
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (or MainThread.InvokeOnMainThreadAsync) is used to dispatch UI work (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync)
frameData.Dispose() is called inside OnSessionUpdated, ideally in a finally block
OnAppearing turns the camera on (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On)) and sets barcodeBatch.Enabled = true
OnDisappearing turns the camera off (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off))
Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>() (or CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>()) is used
ScannedBarcode record is preserved in output
scannedBarcodes list is preserved in output
Deduplication logic is preserved (either as Value-based dedupe like the original, or upgraded to tracking-Identifier-based dedupe via session.AddedTrackedBarcodes + HashSet<int>)
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView()).UseScanditBarcode()
A summary of changes is shown with what was removed and what was added
Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages (Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui)
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription (for iOS target) or Permissions.Camera (for both targets)
